methodology.name: University Research Index (URI)
methodology.aggregation: Per-metric university aggregate = sum of log(1 + x_i) over faculty i
methodology.P: P_term = log(1 + P_S2); P_norm = min-max(P_term) across universities
methodology.size_factor: log(1 + N) where N = faculty count at the university
methodology.missing_values: Non-numeric and missing values are coerced to 0 before aggregation
methodology.s2_priority: Semantic Scholar metrics receive 70% weight vs 30% for Google Scholar in H and C

notes: 151 of 511 authors (29.5%) lack Semantic Scholar data (all S2 metrics zero).
13 universities have fewer than 3 faculty in the dataset.
Google Scholar coverage is generally higher than Semantic Scholar for Cambodian institutions.
URI uses log(1+x) aggregation to reduce sensitivity to single high-performing outliers.
Citations and paper output are min-max scaled; h-index blend (H) is not scaled before weighting.
